rawText:
Fairness is one of those concepts that almost everyone values in the abstract but struggles to define when it comes to the concrete details of everyday life.

Ask residents of any neighborhood what would make their area fairer, and you will likely get answers that range from better schools and cleaner parks to more affordable housing and safer streets.

The variety of responses reveals an important truth: fairness is not a single condition but a cluster of overlapping expectations about access, opportunity, and treatment.

A neighborhood feels fair when its public resources — transportation, green space, health services — are distributed in a way that does not consistently favor one group over another.

This does not mean that every block must be identical; it means that no group should be systematically excluded from the basics that make daily life manageable.

Perceived fairness also depends heavily on process — whether residents feel they have a genuine voice in the decisions that shape their surroundings.

A new development project may be economically sound, yet if it is approved without meaningful consultation, the people most affected are likely to view it as an imposition rather than an improvement.

Conversely, even unpopular decisions can be accepted when the process is transparent and residents believe their concerns were honestly heard, even if those concerns did not change the final outcome.

Trust between institutions and communities is therefore a prerequisite for fairness, not merely a byproduct of it.

When that trust erodes — through broken promises, selective enforcement of rules, or visible neglect of certain areas — residents stop believing that the system works for them, regardless of what the data might show.

Rebuilding that trust requires consistency over time: small, visible improvements that demonstrate commitment, followed by larger projects that deliver on specific promises.

Academics who study urban equity point out that fairness is as much about perception as it is about measurable outcomes, because people evaluate their situation relative to what they see around them, not relative to abstract benchmarks.

A modest park that is well-maintained and genuinely used can contribute more to a sense of fairness than a grand facility that feels designed for visitors rather than for the people who live nearby.

Ultimately, what makes a neighborhood feel fair is not perfection but the credible sense that someone is paying attention and that the benefits and burdens of living together are shared rather than concentrated.

overview.title: What Makes a Neighborhood Feel Fair

sentences.text: Fairness means everyone gets a fair share of good things like parks and schools.
sentences.translation: 공정함이란 공원이나 학교 같은 좋은 것들을 모두가 공평하게 나눠 갖는 거예요.

sentences.text: People want their neighborhood to have clean parks, safe streets, and good schools.
sentences.translation: 사람들은 자기 동네에 깨끗한 공원, 안전한 길, 좋은 학교가 있기를 원해요.

sentences.text: It feels unfair when some areas get nice things and other areas do not.
sentences.translation: 어떤 곳은 좋은 것을 갖고 다른 곳은 못 가지면 불공평하다고 느껴요.

sentences.text: People also want to be listened to when big decisions are made about their area.
sentences.translation: 사람들은 동네에 대한 큰 결정이 내려질 때 자기 말에 귀 기울여 주길 원해요.

sentences.text: When leaders keep their promises, people trust them more.
sentences.translation: 지도자들이 약속을 지키면 사람들은 더 신뢰해요.

sentences.text: A fair neighborhood is one where everyone feels someone cares about them.
sentences.translation: 공정한 동네는 모두가 누군가 자기를 신경 써 준다고 느끼는 곳이에요.

sentences.text: The article explores why fairness is hard to define and what makes a neighborhood feel fair.
sentences.translation: 이 글은 공정함이 왜 정의하기 어렵고, 무엇이 동네를 공정하게 느끼게 하는지 탐구해요.

sentences.text: Fair distribution of public resources like parks, transport, and health services is one key element.
sentences.translation: 공원, 교통, 의료 서비스 같은 공공 자원의 공정한 분배가 핵심 요소 중 하나예요.

sentences.text: Equally important is the process — residents need a genuine voice in decisions that affect them.
sentences.translation: 똑같이 중요한 것은 과정이에요 — 주민들은 자신에게 영향을 미치는 결정에 진정한 목소리를 낼 수 있어야 해요.

sentences.text: Trust between communities and institutions is essential, and it erodes when promises are broken.
sentences.translation: 지역사회와 기관 사이의 신뢰는 필수적이고, 약속이 깨지면 무너져요.

sentences.text: Researchers say fairness is about perception — how people compare their area to what they see around them.
sentences.translation: 연구자들은 공정함은 인식의 문제라고 해요 — 사람들이 자기 지역을 주변과 어떻게 비교하느냐의 문제예요.

sentences.text: The conclusion is that fairness comes not from perfection but from a shared sense that benefits and burdens are spread equally.
sentences.translation: 결론은 공정함은 완벽함이 아니라 혜택과 부담이 고르게 분배된다는 공유된 감각에서 온다는 거예요.

sentences.text: This essay employs an analytical structure to dissect the multifaceted concept of neighborhood fairness, moving from resource distribution to process and trust.
sentences.translation: 이 에세이는 분석적 구조를 사용하여 동네 공정성이라는 다면적 개념을 자원 분배에서 과정과 신뢰로 풀어 나가요.

sentences.text: The writer uses concession markers like "this does not mean" and "conversely" to preempt oversimplification and balance competing claims.
sentences.translation: 글쓴이는 "this does not mean"이나 "conversely" 같은 양보 표지를 사용해 지나친 단순화를 방지하고 경쟁하는 주장들의 균형을 잡아요.

sentences.text: The progression from tangible resources to intangible trust mirrors how fairness research in urban studies has evolved from outcome-based to process-based frameworks.
sentences.translation: 유형의 자원에서 무형의 신뢰로 나아가는 전개 방식은 도시 연구에서 공정성 연구가 결과 기반에서 과정 기반 틀로 진화한 과정을 반영해요.

sentences.text: Rhetorical devices such as the dash for parenthetical examples and the semicolon for parallel refinement keep dense ideas accessible without oversimplifying them.
sentences.translation: 삽입 예시를 위한 대시나 병렬 정제를 위한 세미콜론 같은 수사 기법이 복잡한 아이디어를 단순화하지 않으면서도 접근 가능하게 유지해요.

sentences.text: The contrast between "a modest park" and "a grand facility" grounds the abstract argument in a vivid, relatable image that reinforces the perception-over-measurement thesis.
sentences.translation: "소박한 공원"과 "웅장한 시설"의 대비는 추상적 주장을 생생하고 공감할 수 있는 이미지로 구체화하며, 측정보다 인식이 중요하다는 논지를 강화해요.

sentences.text: The closing sentence synthesizes the essay by redefining fairness as "credible sense" — a deliberately subjective phrase that aligns with the article's argument that perception drives legitimacy.
sentences.translation: 마지막 문장은 공정함을 "믿을 만한 감각"으로 재정의하며 에세이를 종합하는데, 이는 인식이 정당성을 이끈다는 글의 주장에 부합하는 의도적으로 주관적인 표현이에요.

sentences.original: Fairness is one of those concepts that almost everyone values in the abstract but struggles to define when it comes to the concrete details of everyday life.
sentences.translation: 공정함은 거의 모든 사람이 추상적으로는 소중히 여기지만, 일상생활의 구체적인 세부 사항에 이르면 정의하기 어려워하는 개념 중 하나이다.

grammarElements.text: that almost everyone values in the abstract
grammarElements.label: 관계절 1 (제한적)

grammarElements.text: when it comes to the concrete details of everyday life
grammarElements.label: 시간/조건절 (when it comes to)

vocabulary.example: Fairness in education is a widely discussed topic.
The idea sounds good in the abstract.
We need concrete examples, not vague promises.

expressions.usage: 이론이나 원칙 차원에서는 동의하지만 실제 적용에서는 다를 수 있음을 암시할 때 쓴다
특정 주제나 상황으로 논의를 좁힐 때 쓰는 전환 표현

patterns.pattern: [Noun] is one of those [concepts] that everyone [verb A] but [verb B] when it comes to [specific context]
patterns.explanation: 추상적 차원의 동의와 구체적 적용의 어려움을 대비시키며 글의 핵심 긴장을 도입하는 패턴.
examples: Freedom is one of those values that everyone celebrates but struggles to balance when it comes to security.
Equality is one of those ideals that everyone supports but finds hard to implement when it comes to real policy.

drillQuestions.question: Fairness is one of those _____ that almost everyone values.
Everyone values fairness in the _____ but struggles with details.
"When it comes to" introduces a more _____ context.
The word "concrete" here means _____.

sentences.notes: 글의 도입부로, "in the abstract"과 "concrete details"의 대조가 전체 논지의 긴장 구조를 설정한다. "one of those concepts that ~"는 독자의 공감을 유도하는 도입 기법이다.

koreanAlignment.en: that almost everyone values in the abstract
koreanAlignment.ko: 거의 모든 사람이 추상적으로는 소중히 여기지만

koreanAlignment.en: when it comes to the concrete details of everyday life

pronunciationNotes: fairness: /ˈfernəs/ — 첫 음절에 강세. -ness는 약하게 /nəs/로 발음한다.
abstract: /ˈæbstrækt/ — 명사/형용사일 때 첫 음절에 강세. "앱스트랙트"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Ask residents of any neighborhood what would make their area fairer, and you will likely get answers that range from better schools and cleaner parks to more affordable housing and safer streets.
sentences.translation: 어느 동네 주민에게든 무엇이 자기 지역을 더 공정하게 만들지 물어보면, 더 나은 학교와 깨끗한 공원에서부터 더 저렴한 주택과 더 안전한 거리에 이르기까지 다양한 답을 얻게 될 것이다.

grammarElements.text: what would make their area fairer
grammarElements.label: 간접의문문 (what)
목적어 + 관계절 (range from A to B)

vocabulary.example: Residents voiced their concerns at the meeting.
Affordable housing is hard to find in big cities.
Opinions range from support to strong opposition.

expressions.usage: 다양한 범위를 제시할 때 쓰는 표현. 양 극단을 보여주어 폭넓음을 강조한다
명령문 + and 구조로 가정적 상황과 결과를 연결하는 수사적 표현

patterns.pattern: Ask [people] [indirect question], and you will likely get [results that range from A to B]
patterns.explanation: 명령형으로 독자를 가정적 상황에 초대한 뒤, 다양한 반응의 범위를 range from ~ to ~로 제시하는 패턴.
examples: Ask commuters what they need, and you will likely get answers that range from faster trains to cheaper fares.
Ask parents what worries them, and you will likely get answers that range from screen time to bullying.

drillQuestions.question: Ask residents what would make their area _____.
Answers range _____ better schools to safer streets.
"Affordable housing" means housing that is _____.
You will _____ get a variety of answers.

sentences.notes: 명령문 + and 구조("Ask ~ and you will ~")로 독자를 사고 실험에 참여시킨다. "range from A to B"로 공정함에 대한 기대의 다양성을 구체화한다.

koreanAlignment.en: what would make their area fairer
koreanAlignment.ko: 무엇이 자기 지역을 더 공정하게 만들지

koreanAlignment.en: and you will likely get answers
that range from better schools and cleaner parks
to more affordable housing and safer streets
koreanAlignment.ko: 더 저렴한 주택과 더 안전한 거리에 이르기까지

pronunciationNotes: residents: /ˈrezɪdənts/ — 첫 음절에 강세. z 발음에 주의. "레지던츠"에 가깝다.
affordable: /əˈfɔːrdəbl/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "어포더블"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The variety of responses reveals an important truth: fairness is not a single condition but a cluster of overlapping expectations about access, opportunity, and treatment.
sentences.translation: 응답의 다양성은 중요한 진실을 드러낸다: 공정함은 단일한 조건이 아니라, 접근성, 기회, 대우에 관한 겹치는 기대들의 묶음이다.

grammarElements.text: fairness is not a single condition but a cluster of overlapping expectations
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 설명절 (not A but B)

vocabulary.example: A variety of opinions were expressed.
A cluster of problems emerged at once.
The two projects have overlapping goals.

expressions.usage: 잘못된 인식을 부정하고 올바른 정의를 제시할 때 쓰는 대조 구문
분석의 핵심 발견을 도입할 때 쓰는 표현

patterns.pattern: The [noun] of [evidence] reveals [finding]: [concept] is not [A] but [B]
patterns.explanation: 관찰한 증거에서 핵심 통찰을 이끌어낸 뒤, 콜론 뒤에 not A but B로 개념을 재정의하는 패턴.
examples: The pattern of complaints reveals a deeper issue: the problem is not staffing but management.
The range of symptoms reveals a key insight: the illness is not one disease but a spectrum of related conditions.

drillQuestions.question: The variety of responses _____ an important truth.
Fairness is not a single condition _____ a cluster of expectations.
"Overlapping expectations" means expectations that _____.
The colon (:) after "truth" introduces a _____.

sentences.notes: 콜론으로 핵심 정의를 도입하고, "not A but B" 구조로 공정함의 복합적 성격을 밝힌다. "cluster of overlapping expectations"는 이 글의 핵심 논지를 압축한 표현이다.

koreanAlignment.en: fairness is not a single condition
but a cluster of overlapping expectations

pronunciationNotes: variety: /vəˈraɪəti/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "버라이어티"에 가깝다.
overlapping: /ˌoʊvərˈlæpɪŋ/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "오버래핑"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: A neighborhood feels fair when its public resources — transportation, green space, health services — are distributed in a way that does not consistently favor one group over another.
sentences.translation: 동네가 공정하다고 느껴지는 것은, 공공 자원 — 교통, 녹지 공간, 의료 서비스 — 이 한 집단을 다른 집단보다 지속적으로 우대하지 않는 방식으로 분배될 때이다.

grammarElements.text: when its public resources — transportation, green space, health services — are distributed
grammarElements.label: 시간절 (when + 수동태 + 대시 삽입)

grammarElements.text: in a way that does not consistently favor one group over another
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: Resources should be distributed evenly.
The policy consistently favored wealthy areas.
The system should not favor any one group.

expressions.usage: 행위나 상태의 방식을 구체적으로 설명할 때 쓰는 표현
불공평한 비교나 우대를 나타낼 때 쓰는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject] feels [adjective] when [resources] are distributed in a way that does not [verb] [A] over [B]
patterns.explanation: 감각동사 + 형용사로 주관적 인식을 표현하고, when절로 그 조건을 부정 형태로 제시하는 패턴.
examples: A workplace feels fair when opportunities are distributed in a way that does not favor men over women.
A school feels safe when rules are enforced in a way that does not target one group over another.

drillQuestions.question: A neighborhood feels fair when resources are _____ evenly.
The system should not _____ one group over another.
The dash (—) in this sentence adds _____ information.
"Consistently" means happening _____.

sentences.notes: 대시(—)로 공공 자원의 구체적 예시를 삽입하여 추상적 논의를 현실에 연결한다. "does not consistently favor"의 부정 표현이 완벽한 평등이 아닌 체계적 편향의 부재를 기준으로 제시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: that does not consistently favor one group over another
koreanAlignment.ko: 한 집단을 다른 집단보다 지속적으로 우대하지 않는

pronunciationNotes: distributed: /dɪˈstrɪbjuːtɪd/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "디스트리뷰티드"에 가깝다.
consistently: /kənˈsɪstəntli/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "컨시스턴틀리"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: This does not mean that every block must be identical; it means that no group should be systematically excluded from the basics that make daily life manageable.
sentences.translation: 이것은 모든 구역이 동일해야 한다는 뜻이 아니다; 어떤 집단도 일상을 감당할 수 있게 하는 기본적인 것들에서 체계적으로 배제되어서는 안 된다는 뜻이다.

grammarElements.text: that every block must be identical
grammarElements.label: that 명사절 (must + 형용사)

grammarElements.text: that no group should be systematically excluded
grammarElements.label: that 명사절 (should + 수동태)

grammarElements.text: from the basics that make daily life manageable
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: The two plans are not identical.
Some groups have been systematically disadvantaged.
The workload should be manageable for everyone.

expressions.phrase: this does not mean ... it means
expressions.usage: 오해를 미리 차단하고 올바른 해석을 제시할 때 쓰는 양보-명확화 표현
접근이나 참여가 차단된 상태를 나타낼 때 쓴다

patterns.pattern: This does not mean that [extreme claim]; it means that [nuanced claim]
patterns.explanation: 극단적 해석을 먼저 부정한 뒤 세미콜론으로 더 세밀한 정의를 제시하는 양보-정교화 패턴.
examples: This does not mean that all students must score the same; it means that every student should have access to quality teaching.
This does not mean that cities must ban all cars; it means that public transit should be a viable alternative.

drillQuestions.question: This does not mean that every block must be _____.
No group should be _____ excluded from basic resources.
The semicolon (;) connects two _____ ideas.
"Manageable" means something you can _____.

sentences.notes: "This does not mean ~ ; it means ~" 구조로 과도한 해석을 방지하고 논지를 정교화한다. 세미콜론이 부정과 긍정을 대칭적으로 연결하며, "systematically excluded"가 핵심 기준을 제시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: that every block must be identical
it means that no group should be systematically excluded
koreanAlignment.ko: 어떤 집단도 체계적으로 배제되어서는 안 된다는 뜻이다

pronunciationNotes: identical: /aɪˈdentɪkl/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "아이덴티컬"에 가깝다.
systematically: /ˌsɪstəˈmætɪkli/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "시스터매티컬리"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Perceived fairness also depends heavily on process — whether residents feel they have a genuine voice in the decisions that shape their surroundings.
sentences.translation: 인식된 공정함은 과정에도 크게 좌우된다 — 주민들이 자신의 주변 환경을 형성하는 결정에서 진정한 발언권을 가지고 있다고 느끼는지 여부이다.

grammarElements.text: — whether residents feel they have a genuine voice
grammarElements.label: 대시 뒤 동격절 (whether)

grammarElements.text: in the decisions that shape their surroundings
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: Perceived inequality causes social tension.
They wanted a genuine voice in the process.
People want control over their surroundings.

expressions.usage: 어떤 요인이 결과에 큰 영향을 미침을 강조할 때 쓴다
형식적이 아닌 실질적인 참여 기회를 의미한다

patterns.pattern: [Subject] also depends heavily on [factor] — whether [people] feel they have [right/voice] in [decisions]
patterns.explanation: 추가 요인을 제시하고 대시 뒤에 그 내용을 whether절로 구체화하는 패턴. 논점 확장에 쓴다.
examples: Employee satisfaction also depends heavily on culture — whether workers feel they have real input in company decisions.
Voter confidence also depends heavily on transparency — whether citizens feel they have access to reliable information.

drillQuestions.question: Perceived fairness depends heavily on _____.
Residents want a _____ voice in decisions.
"Perceived" means _____ by people.
The dash (—) here introduces a _____ of "process."

sentences.notes: "Perceived fairness"로 공정함이 객관적 사실이 아닌 주관적 인식의 문제임을 강조한다. 대시 뒤 whether절이 "process"의 구체적 의미를 풀어 설명한다.

koreanAlignment.en: in the decisions that shape their surroundings

pronunciationNotes: perceived: /pərˈsiːvd/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "퍼시브드"에 가깝다.
genuine: /ˈdʒenjuɪn/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "제뉴인"에 가깝다. 끝을 "와인"처럼 발음하지 않도록 주의.

sentences.original: A new development project may be economically sound, yet if it is approved without meaningful consultation, the people most affected are likely to view it as an imposition rather than an improvement.
sentences.translation: 새로운 개발 프로젝트가 경제적으로 타당할 수 있지만, 의미 있는 협의 없이 승인된다면, 가장 큰 영향을 받는 사람들은 그것을 개선이 아닌 강제로 볼 가능성이 높다.

grammarElements.text: yet if it is approved without meaningful consultation
grammarElements.label: 양보 + 조건절 (yet if)

grammarElements.text: as an imposition rather than an improvement
grammarElements.label: 보어 (A rather than B)

vocabulary.example: The plan was approved without public consultation.
Residents saw the tax as an imposition.
The argument is economically sound.

expressions.phrase: are likely to view it as
expressions.usage: 사람들의 예상 반응을 추론할 때 쓰는 표현

expressions.phrase: an imposition rather than an improvement
expressions.usage: rather than으로 두 가지 인식을 대비시키는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject] may be [positive], yet if [negative condition], [people] are likely to view it as [A] rather than [B]
patterns.explanation: 긍정적 측면을 양보한 뒤 yet if로 부정적 조건을 제시하고, 그에 따른 부정적 인식을 rather than으로 대비하는 패턴.
examples: A new policy may be efficient, yet if it is imposed without input, workers are likely to view it as control rather than support.
A school merger may save money, yet if it is rushed without explanation, parents are likely to view it as a threat rather than an opportunity.

drillQuestions.question: The project may be economically _____.
Without meaningful _____, people feel excluded.
"An imposition" is something that feels _____.
"Yet" in this sentence signals a _____.

sentences.notes: "may be ~ yet if ~" 구조가 경제적 타당성과 절차적 정당성의 괴리를 드러낸다. "imposition rather than improvement"의 대비가 과정의 중요성을 극적으로 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: A new development project may be economically sound
koreanAlignment.ko: 새로운 개발 프로젝트가 경제적으로 타당할 수 있지만

koreanAlignment.en: yet if it is approved without meaningful consultation
are likely to view it as an imposition

pronunciationNotes: consultation: /ˌkɑːnsəlˈteɪʃən/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "컨설테이션"에 가깝다.
imposition: /ˌɪmpəˈzɪʃən/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "임퍼지션"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Conversely, even unpopular decisions can be accepted when the process is transparent and residents believe their concerns were honestly heard, even if those concerns did not change the final outcome.
sentences.translation: 반대로, 인기 없는 결정이라도 과정이 투명하고 주민들이 자신의 우려가 진정으로 경청되었다고 믿는다면 받아들여질 수 있다 — 그 우려가 최종 결과를 바꾸지 못했더라도.

grammarElements.text: when the process is transparent and residents believe their concerns were honestly heard
grammarElements.label: 시간/조건절 (when + 병렬)

grammarElements.text: even if those concerns did not change the final outcome
grammarElements.label: 양보절 (even if)

vocabulary.example: Low prices attract buyers; conversely, high prices drive them away.
The decision-making process should be transparent.
The final outcome was not what they expected.

expressions.usage: 최악의 경우를 상정하면서도 앞의 주장이 유효함을 나타내는 양보 표현
형식적이 아닌 진심 어린 경청을 강조하는 표현

patterns.pattern: Conversely, even [negative noun] can be [accepted] when [positive condition], even if [limiting clause]
patterns.explanation: 앞 문장과 반대 방향의 논점을 제시하며, even으로 예상 밖의 가능성을, even if로 한계를 인정하는 복합 양보 패턴.
examples: Conversely, even strict rules can be welcomed when they are applied fairly, even if they cause short-term inconvenience.
Conversely, even a pay cut can be tolerated when the reasoning is honest, even if employees disagree with it.

drillQuestions.question: _____, even unpopular decisions can be accepted.
The process must be _____ for people to trust it.
"Even if those concerns did not change the outcome" is a _____ clause.
"Honestly heard" means concerns were _____.

sentences.notes: "Conversely"로 앞 문장의 반대 상황을 제시한다. "even unpopular ~ even if ~"의 이중 양보가 과정적 정당성의 힘을 강력하게 주장한다. 복합적 양보 구조의 모범적 사례이다.

koreanAlignment.en: even unpopular decisions can be accepted
koreanAlignment.ko: 인기 없는 결정이라도 받아들여질 수 있다

koreanAlignment.en: and residents believe their concerns were honestly heard
koreanAlignment.ko: 주민들이 자신의 우려가 진정으로 경청되었다고 믿는다면

koreanAlignment.en: even if those concerns did not change the final outcome
koreanAlignment.ko: 그 우려가 최종 결과를 바꾸지 못했더라도

pronunciationNotes: conversely: /ˈkɑːnvɜːrsli/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "컨버슬리"에 가깝다.
transparent: /trænsˈpærənt/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "트랜스패런트"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Trust between institutions and communities is therefore a prerequisite for fairness, not merely a byproduct of it.
sentences.translation: 따라서 기관과 지역사회 사이의 신뢰는 공정함의 단순한 부산물이 아니라 전제 조건이다.

grammarElements.text: not merely a byproduct of it
grammarElements.label: 대조 보어 (not merely)

vocabulary.example: Trust is a prerequisite for cooperation.
Happiness is often a byproduct of meaningful work.
Public institutions must earn the trust of the people.

expressions.phrase: a prerequisite for, not merely a byproduct of
expressions.usage: 인과관계의 방향을 뒤집어 기존 인식을 교정하는 표현
앞선 논증의 논리적 결론을 제시할 때 쓰는 전환 표현

patterns.pattern: [Noun] is therefore a prerequisite for [X], not merely a byproduct of it
patterns.explanation: 앞선 논증을 종합하며, 일반적 인식("부산물")을 뒤집어 핵심 주장("전제 조건")으로 재정의하는 패턴.
examples: Communication is therefore a prerequisite for teamwork, not merely a byproduct of it.
Respect is therefore a prerequisite for dialogue, not merely a byproduct of it.

drillQuestions.question: Trust is a _____ for fairness.
"Not merely a byproduct" means it is _____ than just a side effect.
"Therefore" signals a _____.
"Institutions" in this context means _____.

sentences.notes: 짧지만 강력한 논지 전환 문장. "prerequisite, not merely a byproduct"로 신뢰와 공정함의 인과관계를 뒤집는다. "therefore"가 앞선 두 문단의 논증을 논리적으로 종합한다.

koreanAlignment.en: not merely a byproduct of it

pronunciationNotes: prerequisite: /priːˈrekwəzɪt/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "프리레퀴짓"에 가깝다.
byproduct: /ˈbaɪprɑːdʌkt/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "바이프로덕트"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: When that trust erodes — through broken promises, selective enforcement of rules, or visible neglect of certain areas — residents stop believing that the system works for them, regardless of what the data might show.
sentences.translation: 그 신뢰가 무너질 때 — 지켜지지 않은 약속, 선별적인 규칙 집행, 또는 특정 지역의 눈에 띄는 방치를 통해 — 주민들은 데이터가 무엇을 보여주든 상관없이 시스템이 자신들을 위해 작동한다고 믿기를 멈춘다.

grammarElements.text: — through broken promises, selective enforcement of rules, or visible neglect of certain areas —
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입구 (수단/원인 나열)

grammarElements.text: that the system works for them
grammarElements.label: that 명사절

grammarElements.text: regardless of what the data might show
grammarElements.label: 양보구 (regardless of + 간접의문문)

vocabulary.example: Public confidence erodes when leaders lie.
Selective enforcement undermines trust.
The visible neglect of the area upset residents.

expressions.usage: 어떤 사실이나 조건과 무관하게 결과가 동일함을 나타낼 때 쓴다
신뢰가 붕괴되는 과정을 묘사할 때 쓴다

patterns.pattern: When [trust/X] erodes — through [cause A], [cause B], or [cause C] — [people] stop [verb-ing], regardless of [counter-evidence]
patterns.explanation: 신뢰 붕괴의 원인을 대시 삽입으로 나열하고, 그 결과를 regardless of로 데이터와 무관한 감정적 반응으로 제시하는 패턴.
examples: When morale erodes — through overwork, broken promises, or unfair promotions — employees stop caring, regardless of what the annual report says.
When credibility erodes — through scandals, cover-ups, or inconsistent messaging — voters stop listening, regardless of what polls predict.

drillQuestions.question: When trust _____, residents lose faith in the system.
Selective _____ of rules is one cause of trust erosion.
"Regardless of" means _____.
"Visible neglect" means neglect that is _____.

sentences.notes: 대시 삽입구로 신뢰 붕괴의 세 가지 원인을 구체적으로 나열한다. "regardless of what the data might show"가 인식과 데이터의 괴리라는 글의 핵심 주제를 강화한다.

koreanAlignment.en: — through broken promises, selective enforcement of rules, or visible neglect of certain areas —
koreanAlignment.ko: — 지켜지지 않은 약속, 선별적인 규칙 집행, 또는 특정 지역의 방치를 통해 —

koreanAlignment.en: that the system works for them
regardless of what the data might show

pronunciationNotes: erodes: /ɪˈroʊdz/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "이로우즈"에 가깝다.
enforcement: /ɪnˈfɔːrsmənt/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "인포스먼트"에 가깝다.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 삽입 나열과 양보 (parenthetical enumeration with concession)

sentences.original: Rebuilding that trust requires consistency over time: small, visible improvements that demonstrate commitment, followed by larger projects that deliver on specific promises.
sentences.translation: 그 신뢰를 재건하려면 시간에 걸친 일관성이 필요하다: 헌신을 보여주는 작고 눈에 보이는 개선이 먼저, 그 뒤에 구체적인 약속을 이행하는 더 큰 프로젝트가 따라야 한다.

grammarElements.text: small, visible improvements that demonstrate commitment
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 구체화 (관계절 포함)

grammarElements.text: followed by larger projects that deliver on specific promises
grammarElements.label: 과거분사구 (순서 제시)

vocabulary.example: Consistency is key to building trust.
The improvements demonstrate real commitment.
The government must deliver on its promises.

expressions.usage: 단발적이 아닌 장기적이고 꾸준한 노력을 강조할 때 쓴다
약속한 바를 실제로 실천함을 나타내는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Gerund phrase] requires [abstract noun]: [concrete examples], followed by [larger steps]
patterns.explanation: 동명사 주어로 과제를 제시하고, 콜론 뒤에 단계적 실행 방안을 구체적으로 제시하는 패턴.
examples: Earning customer loyalty requires patience: quick responses that show care, followed by long-term policies that reward repeat business.
Transforming education requires vision: pilot programs that prove concepts, followed by systemic reforms that scale success.

drillQuestions.question: Rebuilding trust requires _____ over time.
Small improvements _____ commitment.
"Deliver on promises" means to _____ what was promised.
The colon (:) introduces _____ of "consistency over time."

sentences.notes: 동명사 주어(Rebuilding that trust)가 과제를 명사화하고, 콜론 뒤에 "small → larger"의 단계적 처방을 제시한다. "followed by"가 시간적 순서를 명확히 하여 실행 가능한 로드맵을 그린다.

koreanAlignment.en: small, visible improvements that demonstrate commitment
koreanAlignment.ko: 헌신을 보여주는 작고 눈에 보이는 개선이

pronunciationNotes: consistency: /kənˈsɪstənsi/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "컨시스턴시"에 가깝다.
commitment: /kəˈmɪtmənt/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "커밋먼트"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Academics who study urban equity point out that fairness is as much about perception as it is about measurable outcomes, because people evaluate their situation relative to what they see around them, not relative to abstract benchmarks.
sentences.translation: 도시 형평성을 연구하는 학자들은, 사람들이 자신의 상황을 추상적인 기준이 아니라 주변에서 보는 것에 비추어 평가하기 때문에, 공정함은 측정 가능한 결과만큼이나 인식의 문제이기도 하다고 지적한다.

grammarElements.text: that fairness is as much about perception as it is about measurable outcomes
grammarElements.label: that 명사절 (as much A as B)

grammarElements.text: because people evaluate their situation relative to what they see around them
grammarElements.label: 이유절 (because)

vocabulary.example: Urban equity is a growing field of study.
Perception matters as much as reality.
People do not compare their lives to abstract benchmarks.

expressions.phrase: as much about A as it is about B
expressions.usage: 두 요소가 동등하게 중요함을 강조하는 비교 표현
비교 기준을 제시할 때 쓰는 학술적 표현

patterns.pattern: [Experts] point out that [X] is as much about [A] as it is about [B], because [reason]
patterns.explanation: 전문가 인용으로 권위를 세우고, as much A as B로 두 요소의 동등한 중요성을 주장한 뒤, because로 이유를 뒷받침하는 패턴.
examples: Therapists point out that recovery is as much about mindset as it is about medication, because patients who feel hopeful tend to improve faster.
Economists point out that inflation is as much about expectations as it is about supply, because consumers adjust behavior based on what they anticipate.

drillQuestions.question: Academics who study urban _____ research fairness in cities.
Fairness is as much about _____ as it is about outcomes.
"Relative to" means _____.
"Abstract benchmarks" are standards that are _____.

sentences.notes: 글의 핵심 논지를 학술적 권위로 뒷받침하는 문장. "as much about perception as ~ measurable outcomes"가 공정함의 주관적 차원을 학술적으로 정당화한다. "not relative to abstract benchmarks"가 일상적 비교의 중요성을 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Academics who study urban equity point out
that fairness is as much about perception
as it is about measurable outcomes
because people evaluate their situation relative to what they see around them
koreanAlignment.ko: 사람들이 주변에서 보는 것에 비추어 자신의 상황을 평가하기 때문에

pronunciationNotes: equity: /ˈekwɪti/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "에퀴티"에 가깝다. "이퀴티"가 아니다.
perception: /pərˈsepʃən/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "퍼셉션"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: A modest park that is well-maintained and genuinely used can contribute more to a sense of fairness than a grand facility that feels designed for visitors rather than for the people who live nearby.
sentences.translation: 잘 관리되고 실제로 사용되는 소박한 공원이, 인근 주민이 아닌 방문객을 위해 설계된 것처럼 느껴지는 거대한 시설보다 공정함의 감각에 더 많이 기여할 수 있다.

grammarElements.text: A modest park that is well-maintained and genuinely used
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 관계절 (병렬 수동태)

grammarElements.text: more to a sense of fairness
grammarElements.label: 비교급 전치사구

grammarElements.text: than a grand facility that feels designed for visitors
grammarElements.label: 비교 대상 + 관계절

grammarElements.text: rather than for the people who live nearby
grammarElements.label: rather than 대조구

vocabulary.example: A modest investment can yield big returns.
The park is genuinely used by local families.
The grand facility impressed tourists but not locals.

expressions.usage: 비교급과 함께 써서 어떤 것의 효과가 더 크다고 주장할 때 쓴다

expressions.phrase: designed for visitors rather than for residents
expressions.usage: 공공 시설의 실제 수혜자와 의도된 수혜자의 괴리를 나타내는 표현

patterns.pattern: A [modest/small X] that [positive qualities] can contribute more to [goal] than a [grand/large Y] that [negative perception]
patterns.explanation: 소박하지만 실질적인 것이 화려하지만 괴리감 있는 것보다 낫다는 주장을 비교급으로 제시하는 패턴.
examples: A small gesture that is sincere can mean more to someone than a lavish gift that feels obligatory.
A brief meeting that is focused can achieve more than a long conference that feels performative.

drillQuestions.question: A _____ park can contribute more than a grand facility.
The park is well-maintained and _____ used.
The grand facility feels designed for _____ rather than residents.
"Contribute more to a sense of fairness" means to _____ people feel things are fair.

sentences.notes: "modest park"과 "grand facility"의 대비가 인식 중심 논지를 생생한 이미지로 구체화한다. "designed for visitors rather than for the people who live nearby"가 공공 시설의 소외 문제를 날카롭게 포착한다.

koreanAlignment.en: A modest park that is well-maintained and genuinely used
koreanAlignment.ko: 잘 관리되고 실제로 사용되는 소박한 공원이

koreanAlignment.en: can contribute more to a sense of fairness
koreanAlignment.ko: 공정함의 감각에 더 많이 기여할 수 있다

koreanAlignment.en: rather than for the people who live nearby

pronunciationNotes: genuinely: /ˈdʒenjuɪnli/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "제뉴인리"에 가깝다.
facility: /fəˈsɪləti/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "퍼실러티"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Ultimately, what makes a neighborhood feel fair is not perfection but the credible sense that someone is paying attention and that the benefits and burdens of living together are shared rather than concentrated.
sentences.translation: 궁극적으로, 동네를 공정하게 느끼게 하는 것은 완벽함이 아니라, 누군가가 관심을 기울이고 있고 함께 사는 것의 혜택과 부담이 집중되지 않고 공유된다는 믿을 만한 감각이다.

grammarElements.text: what makes a neighborhood feel fair
grammarElements.label: 주어 (what 명사절)

grammarElements.text: and that the benefits and burdens of living together are shared rather than concentrated
grammarElements.label: that 동격절 2 (rather than 대조)

vocabulary.example: Ultimately, trust matters more than data.
The plan needs a credible timeline.
Wealth should not be concentrated in a few hands.
The burdens of living in a city should be shared.

expressions.usage: 비현실적 기준을 배제하고 현실적 기준을 제시할 때 쓰는 대조 표현
공동체 생활의 양면을 포괄적으로 나타내는 짝패 표현
자원이나 부담의 공정한 분배를 나타내는 표현

patterns.pattern: Ultimately, what makes [X] feel [adjective] is not [ideal] but the credible sense that [reality A] and that [reality B]
patterns.explanation: Ultimately로 결론을 열고, what절 주어로 핵심 질문을 다시 던지며, not A but B로 완벽 대신 현실적 기준을 제시하는 패턴. 글 전체를 종합하는 마무리에 적합하다.
examples: Ultimately, what makes a school feel good is not rankings but the credible sense that teachers care and that every child has a chance to grow.
Ultimately, what makes a workplace feel fair is not equal pay but the credible sense that effort is recognized and that opportunities are not reserved for a select few.

drillQuestions.question: _____, what makes a neighborhood feel fair is not perfection.
The answer is not perfection but a _____ sense.
"Benefits and burdens" should be _____ rather than concentrated.
"What makes a neighborhood feel fair" is a _____ clause used as the subject.

sentences.notes: 글 전체의 결론이자 핵심 논지의 종합. "what makes ~ feel fair"로 제목의 질문에 직접 답한다. "not perfection but the credible sense"가 추상적 이상이 아닌 현실적 기준을 제시하며, "shared rather than concentrated"가 분배 정의의 핵심을 압축한다.

koreanAlignment.en: what makes a neighborhood feel fair
and that the benefits and burdens of living together are shared rather than concentrated
koreanAlignment.ko: 함께 사는 것의 혜택과 부담이 집중되지 않고 공유된다는

pronunciationNotes: credible: /ˈkredɪbl/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "크레디블"에 가깝다. incredible과 혼동하지 않도록 주의.
concentrated: /ˈkɑːnsntreɪtɪd/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "칸선트레이티드"에 가깝다.
